meta.source: TCGA lung + HistoTME signatures + recurrence therapy predictions
meta.prediction_scenario: Targeted vs immunotherapy benefit if disease recurs

patients.smoking: Current Reformed Smoker for < or = 15 yrs

targeted_therapy.label: Uncertain benefit
reasons: KRAS G12C: approved targeted options exist but resistance is frequent.

immunotherapy.label: Unlikely benefit
reasons: Immune-desert TME: low T-cell infiltration predicts poor IO monotherapy benefit.
Checkpoint-inhibition signature is elevated on H&E.

patients.smoking: Current Reformed Smoker for < or = 15 yrs

targeted_therapy.label: Unlikely benefit
reasons: No EGFR/ALK/KRAS G12C or other actionable driver: targeted monotherapy unlikely.
Wild-type NSCLC at recurrence is more often IO- or chemo-directed.

immunotherapy.label: Unlikely benefit
reasons: Myeloid/Treg-rich niche is immunosuppressive at recurrence.
Elevated effector/T-cell signatures support IO candidacy.
High Treg/MDSC signals may counteract IO benefit.
No dominant driver: immunotherapy ± chemo is a common recurrence strategy.

targeted_therapy.label: Likely benefit
reasons: Actionable BRAF alteration supports genotype-directed therapy at recurrence.

immunotherapy.label: Unlikely benefit
reasons: Immune-desert TME: low T-cell infiltration predicts poor IO monotherapy benefit.
Prior systemic therapy documented; may affect next-line IO sequencing.
